title: Unless Statement
description: Use the 'unless' keyword to execute code only if a condition is false.
problemStatement:
Negation logic! Assign `false` to a variable `logged_in`. Use an `unless` block to print `"Please log in"` if they are NOT logged in.

**Requirement:** Use the `unless` keyword specifically, not `if !`.
hints: `unless` is like the opposite of `if`. It runs if the statement evaluates to false.
solutionCode:
logged_in = false
unless logged_in
  puts "Please log in"
end
